title: marinellia arvensis
intro: Marinellia arvensis, also known as Field Elm, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of the Marinellia arvensis plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving headaches, and soothing digestive issues. Marinellia arvensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
